Special Weather Statement
National Weather Service Detroit/Pontiac MI
118 AM EDT Fri Jun 12 2026
 
MIZ063-070-120615-
Macomb MI-St. Clair MI-
118 AM EDT Fri Jun 12 2026

...STRONG THUNDERSTORMS WILL IMPACT MACOMB AND ST. CLAIR COUNTIES
THROUGH 215 AM EDT...

At 118 AM EDT, Doppler radar was tracking strong thunderstorms along 
a line extending from near Almont to near Clinton Township to near 
Grosse Ile. Movement was east at 50 mph.

HAZARD...Wind gusts around 50 mph.

SOURCE...Radar indicated.

IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around 
         unsecured objects.

Strong thunderstorms will be near...
  St. Clair Shores, Mount Clemens, New Baltimore, Richmond, Grosse 
  Pointe, Capac, Clinton Township, and Armada around 125 AM EDT.
  Memphis and Emmett around 130 AM EDT.
  Harsens Island around 135 AM EDT.
  Algonac around 140 AM EDT.
  St. Clair, Marysville, and Marine City around 145 AM EDT.
  Port Huron and Lakeport around 150 AM EDT.

Other locations impacted by these storms include Anchorville, North
Street, Roseville, Shelby Township, Lynn Township, Ray Center,
Brockway, Utica, Washington, and Fort Gratiot.

PRECAUTIONARY/PREPAREDNESS ACTIONS...

If outdoors, consider seeking shelter inside a building. 

A Tornado Watch remains in effect until 200 AM EDT for southeastern
Michigan.
